title: User Offboarding Flow
summary: Defines the complete teardown process when a beta/multi-user account holder disconnects from Decifer. Covers position management, credential removal, data export, and Docker container teardown. This spec is intentionally written now so the design is locked before Phase 5 build begins.
preconditions: User has explicitly requested to be offboarded (verbal or written confirmation from Amit)
Market is closed OR user has agreed to force-close any open positions
Amit has been notified and is available to supervise

teardown_steps.how: User (or Amit on their behalf) sends /kill via Telegram or clicks KILL SWITCH on dashboard
teardown_steps.wait_for: All positions confirmed closed — poll ib.portfolio(account) until empty or timeout 10 min
teardown_steps.on_timeout: Escalate to Amit — may require manual close via IBKR Trader Workstation

teardown_steps.how: ib.reqGlobalCancel() — already triggered by /kill at Level 2
Copy data/trades.json, data/orders.json, data/signals_log.jsonl to user-named archive: data/export/<user-id>_<date>.tar.gz
teardown_steps.retention: Archive retained for 7 years for compliance. Not deleted on request.
teardown_steps.note: Immutable audit_log.jsonl (when implemented) is always retained regardless of user request

teardown_steps.how: Remove user's chat_id from config['telegram']['authorized_chat_ids']. Commit change.
teardown_steps.verify: Send /status from removed chat_id — should receive 'Unauthorized' response

teardown_steps.how: Set config['accounts'][user_account_key] = '' or remove key. Commit change.
teardown_steps.note: If this was the active_account, Amit must designate a new active_account before restarting bot

teardown_steps.how: Delete or archive data/settings_override_<user-id>.json if per-user settings exist in Phase 5
teardown_steps.note: In current single-user design, data/settings_override.json is global. Phase 5 must implement per-user scoping before this step is meaningful.

teardown_steps.how: docker stop decifer-<user-id> && docker rm decifer-<user-id>
teardown_steps.note: Only applicable in Phase 5 multi-user Docker deployment. No Docker exists yet.

teardown_steps.how: Amit verbally confirms with the user that their capital has been returned to their brokerage account
Cowork writes to chief-decifer/state/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_offboarding-<user-id>.json confirming all steps completed

open_questions: Are open positions force-closed by Decifer, or handed to user to close manually via their own IBKR login?
Is trade history deleted on user request, or retained indefinitely for compliance? (Current answer: retained.)
In Phase 5 multi-user design, is each user's data isolated in separate files, or merged into shared logs?

data_retention_policy.signals_log: Retained permanently — needed for IC analysis

files_to_build_in_phase_5: scripts/offboard_user.py — automates steps 1-7 with dry-run mode
Per-user data isolation design (separate data/ subdirs or user_id fields in JSON)
